text: The Great Wall of China is not a single continuous wall but many sections built over centuries by different dynasties.
Honey, vinegar, and salt are among the few foods that essentially never spoil when stored properly.
There are more possible ways to shuffle a 52-card deck than there are atoms on Earth.
Bananas are berries by botanical definition, while strawberries are not - they're 'accessory fruits.'
The shortest war in recorded history, between Britain and Zanzibar in 1896, lasted under 45 minutes.
Iceland runs almost entirely on renewable energy, using geothermal and hydropower for most of its electricity and heat.
Venus is the hottest planet in the solar system, hotter than Mercury, because its thick CO2 atmosphere traps heat.
The Eiffel Tower can grow about 15 cm taller in summer as its iron expands in the heat.
A group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance'; their pink color comes from pigments in the algae and shrimp they eat.
The dot over a lowercase 'i' or 'j' is called a 'tittle.'
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire: teaching there dates to 1096, well before Tenochtitlan was founded in 1325.
Sound cannot travel through the vacuum of space because it needs a medium like air or water to carry its vibrations.
